{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Finance and Treasury Officer",
  "description": "Tribest is a corporate support solutions provider with a comprehensive range of people outsourcing services. We have a diversified team of management, strategy, banking and capacity development experts working together to provide organizations with the best human resource management solutions. With a team that diverse, we are setting new standards of performance and raising the bar in excellent service delivery.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance and Treasury Officer

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	The Finance &amp; Treasury Officer is responsible for liquidity management, deposit pricing, financial controls, and accurate financial reporting.
	The role ensures effective management of company funds and strong control over financial risks.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage daily cash position and liquidity
	Price and structure deposits in line with the approved rate card
	Approve deposit tenors and rollover terms
	Maintain the deposit register and ALM discipline
	Match loan disbursements with funding maturities
	Perform bank reconciliations and manage disbursements
	Prepare management accounts and regulatory reports
	Monitor expenses and enforce budget controls


Requirements
Educational Qualification:


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related discipline&nbsp;
	A professional qualification (ACA, ACCA, ICAN) is highly desirable


Experience and Other Requirements:


	4&ndash;7 years of experience in finance, treasury, or banking operations
	Experience in a finance company or lending institution is an advantage.


Skills &amp; Competencies


	Treasury and liquidity management skills
	Understanding of deposit pricing and ALM principles
	Strong accounting and reconciliation skills
	Proficiency in Excel and financial reporting tools
	Strong control mindset and integrity.
",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-23",
  "validThrough": "2026-05-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

    "@type": "Organization",
    "name": "Tribest Corporate Support Limited"

Finance and Treasury Officer Job at Tribest Corporate Support Limited




        
    

        
        Tribest is a corporate support solutions provider with a comprehensive range of people outsourcing services. We have a diversified team of management, strategy, banking and capacity development experts working together to provide organizations with the best human resource management solutions. With a team that diverse, we are setting new standards of performance and raising the bar in excellent service delivery.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Finance and Treasury Officer

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	The Finance & Treasury Officer is responsible for liquidity management, deposit pricing, financial controls, and accurate financial reporting.
	The role ensures effective management of company funds and strong control over financial risks.


Key Responsibilities


	Manage daily cash position and liquidity
	Price and structure deposits in line with the approved rate card
	Approve deposit tenors and rollover terms
	Maintain the deposit register and ALM discipline
	Match loan disbursements with funding maturities
	Perform bank reconciliations and manage disbursements
	Prepare management accounts and regulatory reports
	Monitor expenses and enforce budget controls


Requirements
Educational Qualification:


	Bachelor’s Degree in Accounting, Finance, Economics, or related discipline 
	A professional qualification (ACA, ACCA, ICAN) is highly desirable


Experience and Other Requirements:


	4–7 years of experience in finance, treasury, or banking operations
	Experience in a finance company or lending institution is an advantage.


Skills & Competencies


	Treasury and liquidity management skills
	Understanding of deposit pricing and ALM principles
	Strong accounting and reconciliation skills
	Proficiency in Excel and financial reporting tools
	Strong control mindset and integrity.
    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Commercial Lead Broker",
  "description": "CW Real Estate is a forward-thinking real estate firm redefining the property experience across Nigeria and beyond. We specialize in premium residential and commercial properties, offering tailored solutions combining innovation, expertise, and an in-depth market understanding.

Driven by a passion for excellence, we provide end-to-end services&mdash;from property acquisition and development to sales, leasing, and property management. Our experienced professionals are committed to delivering value, integrity, and results for every client.

At CW Real Estate, we don&rsquo;t just build and sell properties&mdash;we create communities, investments, and lifestyle opportunities that stand the test of time. Whether you&rsquo;re a first-time buyer, seasoned investor, or corporate partner, we&rsquo;re here to help you make smart, informed real estate decisions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Commercial Lead Broker

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	Thelead commercial broker will spearhead CW Real Estate&rsquo;scommercial transactions, focusing onoffice building disposals,office floorsales, commercial leasing,and enterprise-level acquisition mandates.
	This role requires an experienced professional withdeep knowledge of the Lagos commercial propertymarket, particularly within Victoria Island, Ikoyi, Oniru, and Lekki business corridors.
	The ideal candidate is ahigh-performing commercial dealmakerwith a proven record ofclosing high-value commercial real estate transactions, building relationships withcorporate occupiers, institutional investors,and developers, and managing complexasset-level deals.
	The role is critical to strengthening CW Commercial as it expands beyond leasing intostrategic commercial brokerage and enterprise real estate advisory.


Key Responsibilities
Commercial Brokerage &amp; Transactions:


	Lead negotiations and execution of commercial property sales and leasing transactions.
	Handle office building disposals, office floor sales, and commercial asset transactions.
	Manage enterprise acquisition briefs from corporate clients.
	Structure and close high-valuecommercial real estate deals.


Mandate Acquisition:


	Source and secure exclusive commercial property mandatesfrom developers, investors, and institutional owners.
	Identify opportunities for office building sales, portfolio disposals, and commercial acquisitions.
	Position CW Commercial as apreferred brokerage for asset-level transactions.


Enterprise Client Engagement:


	Develop relationships with corporate occupiers, enterprise clients, multinational firms, and institutional investors.
	Provide advisory support to businesses seeking&nbsp;office headquarters, expansion spaces, or investment properties.
	Manage long-term client relationships and strategic mandates.


Market Intelligence &amp; Advisory:


	Maintain strong knowledge of thecommercial real estate landscape in Lagos CBD markets.
	Track trends inoffice demand, investment sales, and commercial leasing activity.
	Advise clients onpricing strategies, investment opportunities, and market positioning.


Collaboration &amp; Internal Coordination:


	Work closely withmarketing and media teamsto promote commercial listings and mandates.
	Coordinate withlegal, operations, and management teamsto ensure smooth transaction execution.
	Support the strategic growth of the CW Commercial division.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor&rsquo;s Degree in Estate Management, Real Estate, Business Administration, Finance, or related fields
	5 - 8 years experience in real estate brokerage, preferably commercial real estate.
	Proven experience handling office sales, commercial leasing, or commercial investment transactions.
	Strong knowledge of Lagos commercial districts (VI, Ikoyi, Oniru, Lekki).
	Demonstrated track record of closing commercial deals.


Preferred Experience:


	Experience working with corporate tenants, developers, or institutional investors.
	Knowledge of commercial property valuation and investment analysis.
	Experience handling asset-level transactions or building disposals.


Added Advantage:


	Existing network of commercial property owners, investors, and corporate tenants.
	Professional certifications inreal estate or property brokerage.
	Strengths or personality evaluation (e.g., CliftonStrengths, DISC, or similar).
	Experience in enterprise-level real estate advisory.


Key Competencies:


	Commercial deal structuring
	High-value negotiation
	Enterprise relationship management
	Market intelligence and investment advisory
	Strategic business development
	Closing complex real estate transactions.


Personal Attributes:


	Highly strategic and commercially minded
	Strong closer with a results-driven mindset
	Professional presence when dealing with corporate clients
	Strong negotiation and persuasion skills
	High level of integrity and professionalism.


Compensation &amp; Benefits


	Competitive salary.
	Benefits (for Full-Time roles): HMO, Pension,company-provided technology Materials.
	Additional Perks: Opportunity for creative freedom, portfolio building with high-value properties, and potential for performance-based bonuses.


Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@cwlagos.com&nbsp;using the Job Position&nbsp; as the subject of the mail.",
  "datePosted": "2026-04-23",
  "validThrough": "2026-06-01",
  "employmentType": "Full-Time",

Commercial Lead Broker Job at CW Real Estate




        
    

        
        CW Real Estate is a forward-thinking real estate firm redefining the property experience across Nigeria and beyond. We specialize in premium residential and commercial properties, offering tailored solutions combining innovation, expertise, and an in-depth market understanding.

Driven by a passion for excellence, we provide end-to-end services—from property acquisition and development to sales, leasing, and property management. Our experienced professionals are committed to delivering value, integrity, and results for every client.
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    



At CW Real Estate, we don’t just build and sell properties—we create communities, investments, and lifestyle opportunities that stand the test of time. Whether you’re a first-time buyer, seasoned investor, or corporate partner, we’re here to help you make smart, informed real estate decisions.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Commercial Lead Broker

Job Location: Ikoyi, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Role Overview


	Thelead commercial broker will spearhead CW Real Estate’scommercial transactions, focusing onoffice building disposals,office floorsales, commercial leasing,and enterprise-level acquisition mandates.
	This role requires an experienced professional withdeep knowledge of the Lagos commercial propertymarket, particularly within Victoria Island, Ikoyi, Oniru, and Lekki business corridors.
	The ideal candidate is ahigh-performing commercial dealmakerwith a proven record ofclosing high-value commercial real estate transactions, building relationships withcorporate occupiers, institutional investors,and developers, and managing complexasset-level deals.
	The role is critical to strengthening CW Commercial as it expands beyond leasing intostrategic commercial brokerage and enterprise real estate advisory.


Key Responsibilities
Commercial Brokerage & Transactions:


	Lead negotiations and execution of commercial property sales and leasing transactions.
	Handle office building disposals, office floor sales, and commercial asset transactions.
	Manage enterprise acquisition briefs from corporate clients.
	Structure and close high-valuecommercial real estate deals.


Mandate Acquisition:


	Source and secure exclusive commercial property mandatesfrom developers, investors, and institutional owners.
	Identify opportunities for office building sales, portfolio disposals, and commercial acquisitions.
	Position CW Commercial as apreferred brokerage for asset-level transactions.


Enterprise Client Engagement:


	Develop relationships with corporate occupiers, enterprise clients, multinational firms, and institutional investors.
	Provide advisory support to businesses seeking office headquarters, expansion spaces, or investment properties.
	Manage long-term client relationships and strategic mandates.


Market Intelligence & Advisory:


	Maintain strong knowledge of thecommercial real estate landscape in Lagos CBD markets.
	Track trends inoffice demand, investment sales, and commercial leasing activity.
	Advise clients onpricing strategies, investment opportunities, and market positioning.


Collaboration & Internal Coordination:


	Work closely withmarketing and media teamsto promote commercial listings and mandates.
	Coordinate withlegal, operations, and management teamsto ensure smooth transaction execution.
	Support the strategic growth of the CW Commercial division.


Required Qualifications


	Bachelor’s Degree in Estate Management, Real Estate, Business Administration, Finance, or related fields
	5 - 8 years experience in real estate brokerage, preferably commercial real estate.
	Proven experience handling office sales, commercial leasing, or commercial investment transactions.
	Strong knowledge of Lagos commercial districts (VI, Ikoyi, Oniru, Lekki).
	Demonstrated track record of closing commercial deals.


Preferred Experience:


	Experience working with corporate tenants, developers, or institutional investors.
	Knowledge of commercial property valuation and investment analysis.
	Experience handling asset-level transactions or building disposals.


Added Advantage:


	Existing network of commercial property owners, investors, and corporate tenants.
	Professional certifications inreal estate or property brokerage.
	Strengths or personality evaluation (e.g., CliftonStrengths, DISC, or similar).
	Experience in enterprise-level real estate advisory.


Key Competencies:


	Commercial deal structuring
	High-value negotiation
	Enterprise relationship management
	Market intelligence and investment advisory
	Strategic business development
	Closing complex real estate transactions.


Personal Attributes:


	Highly strategic and commercially minded
	Strong closer with a results-driven mindset
	Professional presence when dealing with corporate clients
	Strong negotiation and persuasion skills
	High level of integrity and professionalism.


Compensation & Benefits


	Competitive salary.
	Benefits (for Full-Time roles): HMO, Pension,company-provided technology Materials.
	Additional Perks: Opportunity for creative freedom, portfolio building with high-value properties, and potential for performance-based bonuses.


Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position  as the subject of the mail.

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "General Manager (Refractory Products)",
  "description": "Pruvia Integrated Limited, we are general service merchants with a strong desire to ensure customer satisfaction in the midst of efficient and effective service delivery. As an organization we are driven by the dictum of
&lsquo;Impossibility is Nothing&rsquo;.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: General Manager (Refractory Products)

Job Location:&nbsp;Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities&nbsp;


	Oversee the overall management of the refractory products business unit, including operations, sales, procurement, and logistics
	Develop and implement business strategies to grow market share and drive revenue targets
	Manage relationships with key clients, contractors, and industrial partners across sectors such as steel, cement, glass, and petrochemicals
	Monitor production processes to ensure product quality meets industry and client specifications
	Lead procurement of raw materials and ensure cost-effective sourcing without compromising quality
	Develop and manage annual budgets, forecasts, and financial performance reports
	Identify new business opportunities and expand the company&#39;s footprint in the refractory market
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations applicable to refractory operations
	Lead, mentor, and manage a cross-functional team to ensure high performance and accountability
	Liaise with technical teams to resolve product or application challenges at client sites
	Prepare and present regular performance reports to senior management and stakeholders


Requirements


	Bachelor&#39;s degree in Engineering, Materials Science, Industrial Chemistry, Business Administration, or a related field
	Minimum of 6 &ndash; 10 years of experience in the refractory, industrial minerals, or related heavy industry sector
	At least 3 &ndash; 4 years in a senior management or leadership role
	Strong understanding of refractory materials, applications, and market dynamics
	Excellent knowledge of HSE standards within industrial environments
	Strong network within key refractory-consuming industries is a significant advantage.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their&nbsp;CV to:&nbsp;jobpruvia@gmail.com&nbsp; using &quot;General Manager (Refractory Products)&quot; as the subject of the mail.",

General Manager (Refractory Products) Job at Pruvia Integrated Limited




        
    

        
        Pruvia Integrated Limited, we are general service merchants with a strong desire to ensure customer satisfaction in the midst of efficient and effective service delivery. As an organization we are driven by the dictum of
‘Impossibility is Nothing’.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: General Manager (Refractory Products)

Job Location: Ikeja, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Responsibilities 
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Oversee the overall management of the refractory products business unit, including operations, sales, procurement, and logistics
	Develop and implement business strategies to grow market share and drive revenue targets
	Manage relationships with key clients, contractors, and industrial partners across sectors such as steel, cement, glass, and petrochemicals
	Monitor production processes to ensure product quality meets industry and client specifications
	Lead procurement of raw materials and ensure cost-effective sourcing without compromising quality
	Develop and manage annual budgets, forecasts, and financial performance reports
	Identify new business opportunities and expand the company's footprint in the refractory market
	Ensure compliance with health, safety, and environmental regulations applicable to refractory operations
	Lead, mentor, and manage a cross-functional team to ensure high performance and accountability
	Liaise with technical teams to resolve product or application challenges at client sites
	Prepare and present regular performance reports to senior management and stakeholders


Requirements


	Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Materials Science, Industrial Chemistry, Business Administration, or a related field
	Minimum of 6 – 10 years of experience in the refractory, industrial minerals, or related heavy industry sector
	At least 3 – 4 years in a senior management or leadership role
	Strong understanding of refractory materials, applications, and market dynamics
	Excellent knowledge of HSE standards within industrial environments
	Strong network within key refractory-consuming industries is a significant advantage.


Salary
N200,000 - N250,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected]  using "General Manager (Refractory Products)" as the subject of the mail.    

    

        Click here to Apply

{
  "@context": "https://schema.org",
  "@type": "JobPosting",
  "title": "Account Executive",
  "description": "The Concept Group is a holding company for companies established in 1992. Subsidiaries under the Group include: Rosabon Financial Services - Nigeria&rsquo;s Leading Financial Intermediary and Equipment Leasing Company, Concept Nova - Bespoke Enterprise IT Solutions Company, Percy Aitkins - Bureau De Change.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Executive
Job Location:&nbsp;Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary


	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Accounts Executive to manage financial records, support reporting, and ensure compliance with financial standards while providing insights to improve business performance.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare monthly and annual financial reports
	Maintain accurate accounting records and monitor budgets
	Manage cash flow and support cost-reduction initiatives
	Ensure compliance with statutory and regulatory requirements
	Use accounting software and Excel to generate financial data and reports
	Liaise with auditors, banks, and regulatory bodies.


Requirements


	Degree / HND in Accounting, Finance, or related fields
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting standards
	Proficiency in Excel and accounting software
	High attention to detail and strong analytical skills.


Salary
N250,000 - N400,000 per month.&nbsp;

Method of Application&nbsp;
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to:&nbsp;careers@conceptgroup-ng.com&nbsp;using the Job Position as the subject of the email.",

  "validThrough": "2026-07-01",

Account Executive Job at the Concept Group




        
    

        
        The Concept Group is a holding company for companies established in 1992. Subsidiaries under the Group include: Rosabon Financial Services - Nigeria’s Leading Financial Intermediary and Equipment Leasing Company, Concept Nova - Bespoke Enterprise IT Solutions Company, Percy Aitkins - Bureau De Change.

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Account Executive
Job Location: Yaba, Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Job Summary
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	We are seeking a proactive and detail-oriented Accounts Executive to manage financial records, support reporting, and ensure compliance with financial standards while providing insights to improve business performance.


Key Responsibilities


	Prepare monthly and annual financial reports
	Maintain accurate accounting records and monitor budgets
	Manage cash flow and support cost-reduction initiatives
	Ensure compliance with statutory and regulatory requirements
	Use accounting software and Excel to generate financial data and reports
	Liaise with auditors, banks, and regulatory bodies.


Requirements


	Degree / HND in Accounting, Finance, or related fields
	Strong knowledge of financial reporting standards
	Proficiency in Excel and accounting software
	High attention to detail and strong analytical skills.


Salary
N250,000 - N400,000 per month. 

Method of Application 
Interested and qualified candidates should send their CV to: [email protected] using the Job Position as the subject of the email.    

    

        Click here to Apply  
        

    
     




        
        

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});

	This is a full-time role with competitive salary.

Compliance Officer Job at Gbenga Badejo & Co




        
    

        
        Gbenga Badejo and Co. - Our client, a non-bank financial institution specializing in funds management, credit services, financial advisory, portfolio management, and equipment leasing, is recruiting to fill the position below:

We are recruiting to fill the position below:

Job Position: Compliance Officer

Job Location: Lagos
Employment Type: Full-time

Key Responsibilities
     

        



     (adsbygoogle = window.adsbygoogle || []).push({});
    
    




	Review customer onboarding files and credit documentation to ensure full KYC compliance before loan disbursement
	Monitor daily transactions and flag unusual patterns; escalate and file Suspicious Transaction Reports (STRs) where required
	Act as the point of contact for regulatory bodies, including CBN, ensuring timely and accurate filings (returns, reports, queries)
	Maintain and update compliance policies to reflect current regulatory changes and internal process adjustments
	Conduct periodic compliance audits across all departments to identify gaps and enforce corrective actions
	Lead internal investigations into compliance breaches, documenting findings and recommending disciplinary or control measures
	Design and deliver targeted compliance training sessions, especially for frontline teams handling customers and transactions Required Experience


Education and Experience


	A Bachelor Degree or Higher National Diploma in Accounting or related courses from a reputable educational institution.
	4 - 5 years of experience in compliance, risk or audit within a financial institution (lending, microfinance, fintech, or banking) .
	Hands-on experience with AML/CFT frameworks, KYC processes, and transaction monitoring
	Working knowledge of CBN regulations, reporting requirements, and Nigerian Financial Standards.
	Exposure to credit processes and the loan lifecycle within a finance company is strongly preferred.
	Must be a Chartered Accountant.


Compensation


	This is a full-time role with competitive salary.


Note: Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted.
